commit.message:
Libdl: In `Libdl.dlpath`, throw an ArgumentError if the handle is `C_NULL` (#61307)

🤖 Discovered by Codex during an audit.

Other functions in Libdl (such as `Libdl.dlsym`) already include a check
for `C_NULL`. For example:


https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/blob/bc4bb7ae9a49808a929917d921e597906a9f30cb/base/libdl.jl#L59-L60

So this PR adds a `C_NULL` check to `Libdl.dlpath`, and (similar to
`Libdl.dlsym`), throws an `ArgumentError`.

🤖 Co-authored-by: Codex.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: add admin entitlements and shared project improvements

- backend:
  - add admin email allowlist parsing, user-email auth payloads, and a me/entitlements endpoint
  - enforce admin unlimited quota by bypassing exhaustion checks and skipping post-run usage increments
  - keep websocket generation/chat flow aligned with authenticated user context and start-generation args
- frontend:
  - add shared project client flow for /p/[slug] with owner/public modes and read-only behavior
  - add admin entitlement fetch and show unlimited quota labels in dashboard, top bar, and questionnaire
  - preserve chat streaming and pipeline UX while integrating owner/public and quota-state controls
- database:
  - add migrations to make projects public by default and remove client profile self-update policy
- tests:
  - add admin allowlist and generation-service admin quota tests
  - extend generation-start and websocket tests for auth user payloads and entitlements behavior

Closes #17
Closes #19
Closes #31

Co-Authored-By: codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(codex): enforce completion comments via dynamic tools

Summary:
- add a typed Linear issue comment path and require a completion
  comment before a Codex turn is accepted
- advertise and handle dynamic Linear tools using the current
  app-server request and response schema
- update workflow guidance and regression tests for the completion
  comment contract

Rationale:
- prompt-only guidance was not enough to guarantee that completed
  tickets received a Linear summary comment
- the real Codex app-server protocol differs from the earlier fake
  test shape, so the runtime needed schema-compliant dynamic tool
  requests and responses

Tests:
- npm test -- test/codex/app-server.test.ts test/codex/agent-runner.test.ts
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: add context-aware streaming chat agent

- backend:
  - add read-only chat agent prompt/context builder and streaming reply helper
  - replace websocket chat stub with project-owned context loading, streaming events, and stage-completed gate
- frontend:
  - stream assistant deltas into a single growing bubble and finalize on done event
  - disable chat input/send until generation stage is completed and while assistant is replying
  - wire chat disabled/typing props across home, new, and project pages
- tests:
  - add chat agent unit tests for prompt context and streamed reply behavior
  - extend websocket tests for project_id validation, ownership, readiness gate, streaming, and failure paths

Closes #18

Co-Authored-By: codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
cooperator: harden submission spawn-failure completion semantics

Why:
- Submission draining could drop completion signaling when Spawn fails, which can strand SubmitSync callers and leak queued submission ownership under shutdown/allocation-pressure races.

What changed:
- Extended SubmissionEntry with completion-success plumbing for synchronous submit paths.
- Updated SubmitSync to return whether queued work actually spawned and completed.
- Hardened SpawnFromSubmission failure path to destroy entries and release completion semaphores when Spawn fails.
- Hardened DrainRemainingSubmissions to mark synchronous submissions unsuccessful before release.
- Added regression test  using an impossible stack-size request to exercise spawn-failure fast-return behavior.

Validation:
- cmake --build /tmp/coop-build -j 8 --target coop_tests
- /tmp/coop-build/bin/coop_tests --gtest_filter=ShutdownTest.*:SpawnTest.*

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat: backend-owned generation with resilient observability

backend:
- decouple generation execution from websocket lifecycle via HTTP start endpoint and project subscriptions
- add persistent project store/runtime orchestration with generation status, stage, trace id, and incremental artifact writes
- emit detailed coder lifecycle pipeline events with elapsed timing, bounded token usage, and timeout/parse fallback paths
- expand backend test coverage for websocket contracts, generation start endpoint, coder progress, and project store behavior

frontend:
- add ws-aware fallback polling with adaptive backoff for /p/[slug] and stop polling on terminal states
- improve project refresh dedupe so generation-only field changes trigger UI updates
- add terraform progress UX (status text, progress bar, delayed guidance) driven by coder pipeline events
- enrich debug reporting with poll mode and coder milestones and thread new pipeline/terraform progress state through pages

database:
- add generation observability columns and indexes for projects in migration 003_generation_observability

dev:
- exclude virtualenv/cache paths from backend reload watcher to reduce local dev churn

Closes #16

Co-Authored-By: codex <codex@openai.com>

Add Agent Skills for Elastic overview page (#5440)

## Summary

- Adds a new overview page at
`explore-analyze/ai-features/agent-skills.md` describing official
Elastic agent skills for AI coding agents.
- Content based on the
[elastic/agent-skills](https://github.com/elastic/agent-skills) README
and the [Agent Skills](https://agentskills.io/) open standard.
- Covers what agent skills are, scope (Elastic stack coverage),
installation (npx and local clone), supported agents, skill format, and
update workflows.
- Adds the page to the `explore-analyze/toc.yml` under AI-powered
features.

No skill catalogue is included yet — this is the initial overview page
only.

Made with [Cursor](https://cursor.com)

repository.description: Football tracking engine that spatially bins, time-buckets, and flattens Genius Sports JSONL tracking files into compressed Parquet. commit.message:
phase(1): Fix all Codex-identified bugs

## Bugs Fixed (8 total)

### High Severity (2)
- Fix import: refresh_all_decay_scores now implemented
- Fix TTL: extend_ttl() respects expiry, no blind revival

### Medium Severity (3)
- Remove duplicate DecayConfig (single source of truth)
- Use timezone-aware datetime (utcnow helper)
- Fix get_half_life() for non-decay layers (returns None)

### Low Severity (3)
- Fix TTL boundary check (< → <=)
- Improve decay formula (fractional days, clamp access)
- Use relative imports

## Changes
- synapse/layers/types.py: Full rewrite with entities, relations
- synapse/layers/decay.py: Bug fixes + new functions
- synapse/__init__.py: Clean exports
- docs/reports/phase_1_report.md: This report

Report: docs/reports/phase_1_report.md

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(tools): implement Phase 3 default tool suite (#28)

* feat(tools): implement Phase 3 default tool suite

Add 8 built-in tools covering filesystem, shell, and web operations:
- Read, Write, Edit - File operations with workspace scoping
- Glob, Grep - File discovery and content search
- Bash - Shell command execution
- WebSearch, WebFetch - DuckDuckGo search and content extraction

Safety features:
- ToolPolicy for capability switches (all enabled by default)
- Workspace path restriction (no escaping cwd)
- Binary file detection and rejection
- Size limits and truncation with UTF-8 boundary handling

CLI integration:
- Add --deny-write, --deny-bash, --deny-network flags
- Settings file support for tool policy
- Compact JSON result envelopes

New dependencies: ddgs>=4.0.0, trafilatura>=2.0.0

* fix(tools): repair policy overrides and web search wiring

Summary:
- make --deny-* flags override stored tool policy in chat mode
- preserve stored allow_* settings when init rewrites settings.json
- import DDGS from the installed ddgs package and add regression tests
- sync uv.lock with the branch's added web tool dependencies

Rationale:
- one-off deny flags must reliably narrow capabilities even when users
  have permissive defaults stored locally
- init --force should not silently drop persisted tool policy and widen
  later chat runs
- web_search must import the package actually declared by the project

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(web): restore HTML parsing in web_fetch

Summary:
- rename the HTML helper parameter to avoid shadowing the html
  module used for entity decoding
- add a regression test covering HTML title extraction and body
  extraction in web_fetch

Rationale:
- web_fetch raised ToolExecutionError for normal HTML pages because
  html.unescape resolved against a string parameter
- keeping the fix narrow matches the requested scope and leaves the
  other review findings untouched

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_web_tools.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(tools): align tool boundaries with cwd semantics

Summary:
- skip glob and grep entries whose resolved targets escape the
  workspace via symlinks
- clarify that bash starts in cwd but is not sandboxed to it
- add local filesystem regression tests and update the Phase 3 tool
  design notes and system prompt wording

Rationale:
- filesystem discovery tools should treat cwd as both their starting
  point and their effective boundary
- bash cannot honestly promise workspace confinement, so the docs
  need to describe its real contract instead of implying a sandbox

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_local_fs_tools.py tests/unit/test_web_tools.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(theme): persist per-user theme preferences

Summary:
- add shared theme preference models plus cookie and API plumbing for
  per-user theme mode and tint persistence
- apply saved theme classes from app layouts and a client theme
  provider so navigation and reload keep the selected appearance
- update settings controls, auth redirects, and schema docs to support
  light, dark, and system mode across app pages

Rationale:
- body class mutations from the settings page were being lost on page
  changes because layouts always re-rendered with the default theme
- saving theme mode and tint per user lets different accounts keep
  different combinations without sharing a global browser setting
- making theme-sensitive routes dynamic ensures cookie-backed
  rendering stays consistent on pages like /examples as well

Tests:
- npm run typecheck
- npm run build
- Puppeteer runtime validation with mocked /api/me responses covering
  light, dark, system, reload, and cross-user isolation

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: alfab
repository.description: Alfab client monorepo — data platform and automations

commit.message:
fix(circuit-breaker): add reset_circuit_breakers bundle field for manual reset (#21)

When auto_reset_after_minutes=0, there was no way to reset a tripped
circuit breaker without restarting nginx workers. This adds an optional
reset_circuit_breakers field to the policy bundle: when present, apply()
calls circuit_breaker.reset() for each listed limit_key before activating
the new bundle.

Deliberately requires a new bundle deploy (not a direct admin API) so
the free/open-core path has a working but inconvenient reset mechanism.
A SaaS control plane can make this one-click by issuing a bundle with
the reset field pre-populated.

Closes #17

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Cover missing inquirer edge cases

Add regression-style tests for the prompt validation paths that were
still missing in @optique/inquirer, including password and editor
validation failures.

Expand number prompt coverage to include negative values, boundary
values, and decimal-friendly configuration, and add normalization
checks for empty, separator-only, and metadata-heavy choice lists.

Document the current primitive-state annotation behavior with an
explicit test so future refactors have a locked expectation.

Closes https://github.com/dahlia/optique/issues/167

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Summary:
- replace the mixed-language note template with explicit EN and CN file templates
- document the filename convention and pairing workflow for bilingual notes

Rationale:
- keep the template aligned with AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md
- prevent future long-form notes from drifting back into mixed-language files

Tests:
- git diff --check

feat: enforce beta generation quota across backend and UI

backend:
- add a quota service module to read profile limits and increment generations used
- enforce quota checks before websocket generation and return quota_exhausted errors when empty
- increment profiles.generations_used after successful done events with non-blocking error logging

tests:
- add websocket handler tests for quota exhaustion, successful increments, and no increment on pipeline failure
- update generation pipeline test setup to stub quota checks and avoid external profile access

frontend:
- load profile quota from Supabase on app load and refresh quota after successful generation
- show remaining generation quota in the top bar and on the questionnaire generate screen
- disable Generate Architecture at zero remaining and show beta exhaustion messaging
- stop generating state when websocket error responses arrive

Closes #12

Co-Authored-By: codex <codex@openai.com>

Relax inquirer JSDoc wording assertions

and the key cancellation identifier instead of matching narrative
prose. That keeps the tests stable while still enforcing the
documentation contract from the style guide.

commit.message:
Use file paths for inquirer JSDoc fixture reads

Convert the inquirer JSDoc regression tests to resolve the source
file to a path string before calling readFileSync(). This avoids
relying on URL support details in node-compat layers across Deno,
Node.js, and Bun.

https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/171#discussion_r2929633489

commit.message:
Use file paths for env JSDoc fixture reads

Convert the env JSDoc regression tests to pass a filesystem path
string into readFileSync() instead of a URL object. This keeps the
test aligned with the repo's cross-runtime constraints and avoids
node-compat differences around URL handling.

https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/171#discussion_r2929633449

commit.message:
Anchor inquirer JSDoc extraction to declarations

Make the inquirer JSDoc regression helper locate the target
declaration first and then read only the adjacent preceding
doc block. This removes over-capture from earlier comments and
keeps the matcher portable across LF and CRLF checkouts.

https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/171#discussion_r2929597506
https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/171#discussion_r2929603391

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: optique
repository.description: Type-safe combinatorial CLI parser for TypeScript

commit.message:
Anchor env JSDoc extraction to declarations

Make the env JSDoc regression helper find the target declaration
first and then read only the adjacent preceding doc block. This
avoids false positives from earlier comments and tolerates CRLF
line endings via trailing-whitespace matching.

https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/171#discussion_r2929597485
https://github.com/dahlia/optique/pull/171#discussion_r2929603389

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Document thrown errors in env and inquirer

commit.message:
검토 도메인 액션 API 및 권한 스켈레톤 추가 (#4)

* 검토 도메인 엔티티 추가

* 검토 추가 검토자 모델과 에러코드 추가

* 검토 임시 권한 컨텍스트와 포트 추가

* 검토 액션 API 스켈레톤과 DTO 추가

* 검토 액션 서비스를 유스케이스별로 분리

* 검토 액션 정책 테스트 추가

* 검토 업무 버전 필드 제거

* 검토 명령 서비스를 단일 서비스로 단순화

* feat: add korean swagger docs for review APIs

* fix(Review)Entity 불변식 검증 추가

private 생성자 추가
생성자 컨벤션 맞춤

* refactor(review): separate service contracts from controller dto

* refactor(review): remove lock version from additional reviewer api

* refactor(review): Task 연관 대신 taskId를 사용하도록 변경

- Review 엔티티가 Task 연관 대신 taskId만 보관하도록 수정\n- Review 저장소와 서비스가 taskId 기준으로 조회하도록 정리\n- 커밋과 PR 메시지 한글 작성 규칙 문서 추가

* fix(review): 반려 사유 입력과 도메인 검증을 함께 적용

- 반려 요청 DTO에 길이 제한 검증 추가\n- Review 엔티티에 반려 사유 최종 검증 추가\n- 내부 검증 실패를 VALIDATION_ERROR로 응답하도록 예외 처리 추가

* Delete src/test/java/com/example/workmanagement/domain/review/service/ReviewCommandServiceTest.java

* refactor(review): ReviewComment 정적 팩토리를 일반 생성자로 단순화

* refactor(review): 엔티티 생성 패턴을 일관되게 정리

- ReviewHistory는 private 생성자와 정적 팩토리 조합으로 정리\n- ReviewReference, ReviewAdditionalReviewer, ReviewAttachment는 일반 생성자로 단순화\n- 서비스 생성 호출부를 엔티티 생성 패턴에 맞게 정리

* docs: CONTRIBUTING 문서를 제거하고 AGENTS로 통합

* refactor(review) : global 공통 코드 반영

global 공통 코드 반영

error 도메인 별로 분리
각 에러가 적용되는 코드들 수정

* refactor(review) dev 브랜치와 동일하게 세팅

* Create docker-compose.yml

* Delete monitoring/logs/app-json.log

* Delete src/test/resources/application.properties

* task repo 삭제

* Update application.yml

* fix(review) : 롬복 getter 반영, 컴파일 에러 수정

컴파일 에러 방지를 위해 임시 task 추가 주석으로 달아놓음

getter 반영

* fix(refactor) : 임시 task 경로 이동

MockTask로 review 하위로 경로 이동

* fix(review) : import 문제 해결

task -> 위치 옮기면서 발생한 import문제 해결

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: minji0828 <minji0828@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(cli): restore and harden session/settings migration (#27)

* fix(settings): support nested env/defaults structure and new env var names

- Rename settings file from setting.json to settings.json (with legacy fallback)
- Support nested structure with 'env' and 'defaults' sections
- Add support for AGENTLET_API_KEY and AGENTLET_BASE_URL env vars
- Maintain backward compatibility with flat structure and OPENAI_* env vars
- Add comprehensive tests for new functionality
- Extract shared helpers for validation and settings construction
- Add change detection for os.environ mutations

Fixes settings loading to work with user's ~/.agentlet/settings.json format.

* test: add explicit monkeypatch cleanup for env vars in settings tests

Add monkeypatch.delenv() calls before load_settings() to ensure proper
cleanup of environment variables set during tests. This makes the test
isolation explicit and prevents potential pollution of the global env state.

* refactor(settings): remove env section support from settings.json

- Remove _apply_env_section() function that loaded env vars from settings
- Simplify _load_nested_settings() to only handle defaults section
- Update load_settings() to detect nested structure by 'defaults' key only
- Remove tests for env section functionality
- Remove non-existent inject_project_env import from test script

BREAKING CHANGE: The 'env' section in settings.json is no longer supported.
Environment variables should be set externally (e.g., in shell profile).

* refactor(settings): clarify docstring for load_settings

Remove the confusing mention of 'env' section no longer being
supported since this feature was never implemented.

* feat(settings): add LiteLLM-standard provider-specific env var resolution

- Add provider-to-env mappings for API keys and base URLs following
  LiteLLM naming conventions (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, AZURE_API_KEY, etc.)
- Unify _resolve_api_key and _resolve_api_base into single
  _resolve_provider_env function with keyword parameters
- Normalize provider name once to avoid redundant .lower() calls
- Add comprehensive tests for provider-specific env var resolution
- Maintain backward compatibility with OPENAI_API_KEY as fallback

Priority order:
1. AGENTLET_API_KEY (project-specific override)
2. {PROVIDER}_API_KEY (LiteLLM standard)
3. OPENAI_API_KEY (backward compatibility)
4. settings.json value

* chore: add AI coding agent directories to .gitignore

* refactor(sessions): migrate session storage to ~/.agentlet

Migrate session and history storage from {cwd}/.agentlet/ to ~/.agentlet/
to make agentlet behave more like a standard Python tool.

Changes:
- Sessions stored at ~/.agentlet/sessions/{cwd_hash}/
- History stored globally at ~/.agentlet/history
- Added get_data_dir() and _cwd_hash() helper functions
- Updated SessionStore to accept optional data_dir parameter
- Added pytest fixture to reduce test boilerplate
- Updated documentation for new storage paths

The cwd_hash is the first 16 chars of MD5 hash of the resolved cwd path,
providing isolation between projects while keeping data centralized.

* docs: add comprehensive CLI usage guide and update README

- Complete rewrite of README with quick start, configuration, and CLI usage
- New docs/cli-usage.md with detailed guide covering:
  - Settings file and environment variable configuration
  - Provider-specific setup for OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, etc.
  - Command reference for init and chat commands
  - One-shot vs interactive mode examples
  - Session management documentation
  - Troubleshooting guide
- Updated design doc with configuration management section
- Added documentation section to README with links to all guides

* chore: update default model from gpt-4o-mini to gpt-5.4

Update all references to use gpt-5.4 as the default model:
- README.md examples and configuration table
- docs/cli-usage.md examples and settings
- Design docs default references
- DEFAULT_MODEL constant in registry.py
- Test fixtures and assertions

* fix(provider): support documented LiteLLM backends

Summary:
- register all documented LiteLLM-backed provider names in the runtime
- align real API helpers with exported env vars and ~/.agentlet/settings.json
- add registry coverage for supported provider names and unknown-provider errors

Rationale:
- the docs now advertise non-OpenAI providers, so the runtime must accept
  those provider names instead of failing at chat startup
- the real API helpers should reflect the current configuration model rather
  than referring to removed .env loading behavior

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_provider_registry.py
  tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py tests/unit/test_cli_settings.py
  tests/smoke/test_cli_chat.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(cli): restore session and settings migration paths

Summary:
- add fallback and migration logic for legacy project-local sessions
- allow `--session` to load transcripts across cwd hash buckets
- write future config updates to the canonical settings.json path

Rationale:
- preserve resume behavior for users upgrading from the old storage layout
- make explicit session IDs work with the new home-scoped session store
- complete the settings filename migration without breaking legacy reads

Tests:
- uv run pytest

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* refactor(cli): remove --session flags and CLI env var overrides

Remove the ability to pass --session, --continue, --new-session flags
and --provider, --model, --api-key, --api-base, --temperature,
--max-tokens overrides via CLI. Configuration now comes only from
~/.agentlet/settings.json or environment variables.

Changes:
- Remove session resume functionality from chat command
- Remove CLI argument overrides for provider settings
- Simplify chat_app.py by removing load_session_for_resume()
- Add _create_provider_config() helper for type-safe config creation
- Move SessionStore creation inside interactive mode block
- Update tests to reflect simplified CLI interface
- Update documentation in README.md and cli-usage.md

This simplifies the CLI surface and establishes a clearer
configuration hierarchy: env vars > settings file > defaults.

* fix(cli): restore chat resume and override flags

Summary:
- restore `chat` parser support for session resume flags and per-call
  provider settings overrides
- wire interactive resume back through the home-scoped session store
  and show resumed session state in the REPL header
- add regression coverage for parser acceptance, resumed sessions, and
  one-shot override propagation

Rationale:
- preserve the existing CLI contract expected by users, tests, and the
  updated documentation
- keep the new `~/.agentlet` migration path reachable from the actual
  CLI instead of leaving it stranded behind removed flags

Tests:
- uv run pytest

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* refactor(cli): remove session resume flags and CLI env var overrides

- Remove --continue, --session, --new-session flags from chat command
- Remove AGENTLET_API_KEY, AGENTLET_BASE_URL, AGENTLET_PROVIDER, AGENTLET_MODEL env vars
- Simplify settings to only use settings.json and built-in defaults
- Prevent stale credentials when overriding provider via CLI
- Update all documentation to reflect new configuration approach
- Update tests to match new behavior

* fix(cli): harden session hashing and migration

Summary:
- replace MD5 cwd hashing with truncated SHA-256 session bucket hashes
- bound cross-bucket session lookup and warn on duplicate session ids
- make legacy migration writes atomic and type CLI setting overrides

Rationale:
- avoid weak hashing primitives in filesystem path generation
- keep session resolution deterministic when the same id appears twice
- prevent concurrent migration writers from overwriting each other

Tests:
- uv run pytest tests/unit/test_sessions.py tests/unit/test_cli_surface.py

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: fvm use 대화형 프롬프트 차단 (#6)

* fix: 빌드 준비 단계 불필요 작업 최적화

- fresh clone 경로를 shallow clone으로 축소했습니다.
- melos workspace 여부에 따라 pub 명령을 분기했습니다.
- iOS pod install은 실패 시에만 repo update로 재시도합니다.
- Android 전용 빌드에서는 iOS precache를 생략합니다.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: fvm use 대화형 프롬프트 차단

- fvm use를 강제 비대화형 옵션으로 실행합니다.
- melos 설정 질문으로 빌드가 멈추지 않도록 테스트를 추가했습니다.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: GitHub action 웹훅과 shorebird 빌드 흐름 정리 (#4)

* fix: 로컬 CI/CD 파이프라인 구조와 온보딩 정리

- Python 중심 오케스트레이션과 단계 가시성 구조로 리팩토링
- 대시보드와 진단 화면을 강화하고 설치 진입점을 단순화
- 처음 pull 받은 뒤 따라갈 수 있는 한글 가이드를 추가

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: GitHub 액션 엔드포인트와 웹훅 정책 정리

- GitHub action 경로를 /github-action/* 기준으로 정리
- Shorebird patch를 GitHub 시그니처 검증 흐름으로 통합
- develop -> main 머지 기반 prod 트리거 정책과 테스트 추가

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore: dev 웹훅 브랜치 prefix 규칙 조정

- WEBHOOK_DEV_HEAD_PREFIX 기본값을 release/dev로 변경
- 관련 문서와 템플릿 예시를 현재 규칙에 맞게 정리

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: 웹훅 정책을 원래 운영 규칙으로 복원

- dev는 release/dev* -> develop 머지일 때만 트리거
- prod는 main 기준 태그 생성일 때만 트리거
- 과한 prod PR 머지 규칙과 관련 설정 제거

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore: 레거시 웹훅 엔드포인트 제거

- /webhook alias를 제거하고 GitHub action 경로만 유지
- API 문서와 설정 가이드를 새 경로 기준으로 정리

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* chore: env.sample 제거

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: dev 빌드 웹훅 기준을 release/dev 머지로 변경

- dev는 release/dev* 브랜치로 머지될 때만 트리거
- 관련 env 키를 WEBHOOK_DEV_BASE_PREFIX로 단순화
- 문서와 테스트를 새 운영 규칙에 맞게 정리

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

* feat: shorebird 태그 트리거와 수동 패치 빌드 추가

- github-action/shorebird는 prod 태그 생성 시에만 빌드 트리거
- /build/shorebird 수동 엔드포인트를 추가해 shorebird patch 빌드 실행 지원
- 관련 문서와 테스트를 새 동작에 맞게 정리

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(sdk): expand Direct API coverage and align core contracts

Summary:
- add service support for ad images, agency clients, bid modifiers,
  dictionaries, keyword bids, keywords, and sitelinks
- align core Ads, Campaigns, and AdGroups behavior closer to the
  official API surface, including broader Ads payload support
- add explicit request apiVersion support for official v5 and v501
  service endpoints
- extend README and contract tests to cover the expanded SDK surface

Rationale:
- move the SDK from MVP coverage toward broader Yandex Direct API
  coverage while keeping the transport and error model consistent
- remove non-official AdGroups methods and reflect official endpoint
  versioning in the public request options

Tests:
- npm run build
- npm test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(sdk): add missing Yandex Direct service methods

Summary:
- add missing Campaigns methods for delete, archive, and unarchive
- add Ads methods for delete, archive, unarchive, and moderate
- add AdGroups delete and expose ads/adGroups on the public client
- update README and tests to cover the expanded SDK surface

Rationale:
- align the implemented SDK methods with the official service method sets
- keep transport behavior and response contracts validated by tests

Tests:
- npm run build
- npm test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

docs: bootstrap symphony playbook repository

Summary:
- add a README focused on OpenAI Symphony setup notes and field logs
- add the first note covering Linear/Symphony review and rework handling
- add lightweight cases, templates, and bootstrap planning docs

Rationale:
- start with a logbook-first repository that matches the current exploration stage
- preserve the initial workflow question and the code-backed conclusions for future reuse

Tests:
- git diff --cached --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Run bot every 6 hours

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat: Bab plugin adapters — initial release

Delegate plugin adapters for AI CLI agents — Claude Code, Codex,
Copilot, and OpenCode. Each implements the SimpleAdapter interface
(buildCommand + parseResult + discover) for the bab MCP server.

Claude: stdin-based prompting, CLAUDE_* env stripping, permission
mode support, JSON output parsing with cost/token extraction.

Codex: JSONL output parsing with agent_message extraction, profile
and model flags, CODEX_* env stripping, web_search capability.

Copilot: JSON event stream parsing (assistant.message, text, result),
dynamic model discovery via --help, --allow-all is an opt-in via
role args (not set by default).

OpenCode: JSON output parsing, configurable model/provider flags,
role-based prompt files.

All adapters use Set-based flag allowlists and array-based command
building (no shell injection possible).

Co-authored-by: claude-opus-4-5 <claude-opus-4-5@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot <copilot@github.com>

repository.name: plugins
repository.description: First-party delegate plugins for Bab — copilot, codex, and claude adapters

commit.message:
feat: Bab MCP server — initial release

Bab is an MCP server that delegates AI coding tasks to CLI agents
(Claude Code, Codex, Copilot, OpenCode) via a plugin system,
powered by Vercel AI SDK across Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and OpenRouter.

Core features:
- MCP server with 17 tools: delegate, consensus, chat, analyze, debug,
  codereview, refactor, testgen, docgen, secaudit, thinkdeep, tracer,
  planner, precommit, challenge, list_models, version
- Plugin system: manifest-driven, adapter-based, dynamic import with
  realpath containment, Zod-validated manifests, atomic install/remove/list
  with staging and rollback
- ModelGateway: unified AI SDK routing across Anthropic, OpenAI, Google,
  OpenRouter, and plugin-hosted models (pluginId/modelName)
- Consensus tool: multi-model routing, parallel execution, per-model
  temperature and thinking_mode
- Agent Skills generation: auto-discovers host agents, writes SKILL.md
  with tool catalog and plugin references
- Selfupdate: fail-secure with SHA256 checksum verification, origin
  validation, and atomic binary replacement with rollback
- Install script: platform detection, checksum verification, brew fallback,
  sudo escalation messaging
- SDK + test-utils exported for plugin authors

Security:
- Copilot --allow-all is an opt-in via role args
- Plugin install trust warning + SECURITY.md with disclosure policy
- Array-based spawn (no shell injection), realpath containment
- Env stripping: PATH, LD_PRELOAD, DYLD_INSERT_LIBRARIES, NODE_OPTIONS,
  BAB_INTERNAL_* blocked
- YAML billion-laughs protection (maxAliasCount: 10)
- Timeout validation, GHA injection prevention

Tests:
- 211 unit tests + 8 integration tests (hermetic harness, isolated HOME,
  minimal ENV_PASSTHROUGH)
- E2E test for OpenCode plugin with stub binary
- CI: unit and e2e steps run separately

Co-authored-by: claude-opus-4-5 <claude-opus-4-5@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: copilot <copilot@github.com>

repository.name: bab
repository.description: A TypeScript MCP server built on Bun — gateway between MCP clients and AI CLI tools
